pattern: Minimizing a rearranged number is a digit-frequency argument in disguise: place digits from smallest to largest, and the only wrinkle is dodging a leading zero (or, for a negative sign, running the same argument backwards to maximize the magnitude).
hints: Work only with the digits of the absolute value; the sign just decides which direction of 'best' you want at the end.
For a non-negative result, sorting the digits ascending is almost right, except a leading zero has to be swapped out for the smallest nonzero digit available.
For a negative result, the most negative number is the one whose digits are sorted descending -- there is never a leading-zero problem in that direction.

edge_inputs.description: zero has no digits to rearrange
leading zero would appear without the swap-in fix
two-digit number needing the leading-zero fix
negative counterpart of the leading-zero trap, which needs no fix
all digits identical, rearrangement is a no-op

stress.description: random signed values spanning short to maximum-length digit strings
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    magnitude = rng.randint(0, min(size, 1000000000000000))
    sign = rng.choice([1, -1])
    return [sign * magnitude]
